title: llm-memory-eval: A Reproducible Evaluation Harness for Summarization-Based Memory and Retrieval-Augmented Generation in Large Language Models
description: Open-source Python package operationalizing a controlled, within-instance, repeated-measures comparison of Summarization-Based Memory and Retrieval-Augmented Generation for long-term conversational performance in large language models, evaluated on the LongBench, LoCoMo, and LongMemEval benchmarks. Provides backend-agnostic inference (Ollama, Together AI, AWS Bedrock, Hugging Face Inference Endpoints, OpenAI-compatible), deterministic decoding controls, and a complete statistical pipeline (paired-samples t-tests with Holm-Bonferroni correction, Wilcoxon signed-rank, two-way ANOVA on Strategy x Conversation Length with Bonferroni-corrected simple effects, Shapiro-Wilk and Levene diagnostics, Cohen's d, and partial eta-squared).

creators.name: Okeke, Abuchi
creators.affiliation: University of the Cumberlands, Williamsburg, Kentucky, USA
